channel.title: Bioinformatics Blogs
channel.description: A feed of the latest posts of Bioinformatics blogs around the world

item.title: RNA-seq analysis in seconds using GPUs
item.description: A new GPU implementation of kallisto enables RNA sequencing analysis in seconds, accelerating transcript quantification and dramatically reducing computation time for large transcriptomic datasets.

item.title: Research Advisor – Computational RNA Genomics
item.description: A Lilly Research Advisor position highlights the growing importance of RNA sequencing and bioinformatics for analyzing transcriptome data and advancing genetic medicine programs and therapeutic develo

item.title: Weekly Recap (March 13, 2026)
item.description: Phil Bourne, Anthropic Institute, eLife, AI@UVA / AI in Virginia, NIH genomics tech, AIxBio, AI+Rstudio, legibility, how scientists use Claude Code, how to design antibodies, R updates, new papers.

item.title: Capsule technology opens new window into individual cells
item.description: A new capsule-based platform enables repeated analysis of individual cells while preserving genetic material, combining single-cell workflows with RNA sequencing to study rare cell types and cellular

item.title: Crop Diagnostix launches RNA-based crop health early-warning system
item.description: RNA reveals plants' real-time biological response to stress, which occurs long before conventional tools detect problems, says the firm. https://youtu.be/xyM1HbzHuG8 A visual inspection of crops may

item.title: RNA sequencing advances are improving cell lineage tracing and reconstruction of cell fate decisions
item.description: Over the past two decades, cell biologists and computational biologists have worked to develop lineage tracing techniques that can decode the relationships between progenitor cells and their different

item.title: From Protocol to Pipette: What Two New RCTs Tell Us About AI Biosecurity Risk
item.description: LLMs uplift novice performance on computational biology tasks, while their effect in physical laboratories remains modest and task-dependent.

item.title: Roche Axelios1 Run Pricing: Not A Knockout - Yet?
item.description: By far the most awaited news at AGBT was Roche's reveal of the pricing scheme for Axelios 1 consumables: what would be the structure and how much would different runs cost? Indeed, Roche sequencing s

item.title: Remembering Phil Bourne
item.description: Personal reflections on a legendary scientist, leader, builder, and mentor

item.title: Structured AI Integration as Quality Control for Peer Review
item.description: Decades of research shows that inter-rater reliability is low in grant/manuscript reviews. A new paper presents a case for institutional AI integration in peer review.

item.title: Complete Genomics Sale: Gruyère or Emmental?
item.description: A bit of interesting news during AGBT was the announcement of the sale of Complete Genomics to Swiss Rockets, a Basel-based company which had<a href="https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/mgi-tech-

item.title: Weekly Recap (March 6, 2026)
item.description: Biology essays, fast bio bounty, PDF accessibility in Quarto, Claude Skills for R, sex lives of neanderthals and humans, Ginkgo cloud lab, Applied Machine Learning Conference, AI in Science

item.title: Ultima Genomics Solaris 2.0: Greater Version, Smaller Beads, Lose A Box
item.description: Ultima Genomics announced the 2.0 version of their Solaris chemistry at AGBT. Perhaps the biggest splash here is that Solaris 2.0 relies on an isothermal amplification chemistry operating on smaller

item.title: Furtress of solitude
item.description: In the Pilbara region of Western Australia lives the western pebble mouse ( Pseudomys chapmani ), a rarely seen rodent that excavates a complex subterranean burrow system in the rocky substrate. These

item.title: The Mentorship Index
item.description: Background

item.title: Element Previews High-Throughput VITARI
item.description: On the Thursday before AGBT, Element Biosciences unveiled their VITARI high throughput sequencer, expected to ship in the second half of this year. VITARI surprised much of the sequencing community,

item.title: Illumina Constellation Becomes TruPath Genome
item.description: <div>Illumina is rebranding their Constellation mapped read technology to TruPath Genome and launching it into the marketplace. Priced at $395 per genome, TruPath Genome was presented as being superio

item.title: Using MOB-suite to predict plasmids in bacterial genome assemblies
item.description:
Today I wanted to predict plasmids in a bacterial genome assembly, and used the MOB-suite tool.

Here's how I ran it on the Sanger

item.title: Creating custom (non-Font Awesome) icons for a Jekyll-based website

item.title: State of the Blog
item.description: ChatGPT came out in November 2022 and it has completely changed the Internet. In my post Learning about ChatGPT via ChatGPT I had prompted: "Will ChatGPT replace Google?" to which the model back th

item.description:

item.description: When using native barcoding kits from ONT we have noticed some low frequency miss-assignments between barcodes/samples. Now we don’t know if this is an issue from the wet lab side (cross cont

item.title: Video games in 2025: puzzles galore
item.description:
I played more games this year, particularly puzzle games. I also just bought a Switch 2, which seems good so far (nice screen).

Puzzle games

item.title: cfDNA fragmentomics reviewed
item.description: This is a fantastic and comprehensive review on Cell-free DNA fragmentomics in cancer. Out in Cancer Cell by Denis Lo et al. In this review focused on cell-free DNA (cfDNA) fragmentomics

item.title: 15th Anniversary
item.description: It has been 15 years since I started this blog! As typical with many of my anniversary posts, I reflect on things that I have started to do and/or have learned since the last time I wrote an annive

item.title: The case of the missing Editorial Blog Post (and journal team)
